title: EvidenceReliability
description: Schema for an evidence reliability scoring entry. Records the source type, reliability tier, confidence, verification status, and expiry for external evidence consumed by the AI-native control plane. Aligned with docs/ai-native/evidence-reliability-policy.md and docs/ai-native/external-reality-intake.md.

sourceType.description: Source class of the external evidence, per external-reality-intake.md classification rules.

score.description: Reliability tier. A = authoritative primary source, B = trusted secondary, C = community/heuristic, D = unverified or stale. See evidence-reliability-policy.md.

confidence.description: Confidence in the reliability score assignment, from 0 (guess) to 1 (certain).

verificationStatus.description: Whether the evidence has been verified. verified = machine-checked or version-pinned. cross-verified = two independent sources agree. unverified = no verification performed. conflicting = sources disagree.

capturedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of when this reliability entry was recorded.

expiresAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp after which this evidence is considered stale. Null if no expiry applies. Sources older than 12 months without version pin should have an expiry set.

sourceUrl.description: Canonical URL of the evidence source, if available.

versionPinned.description: Dependency version the evidence is pinned to (e.g. installed package version). Null if not version-specific.

staleReason.description: Reason the evidence is considered stale, if applicable (e.g. 'source older than 12 months', 'capturedAt > 72 hours ago').

taskGateResult.description: Gate outcome for an execution task type. null if not yet evaluated. See evidence-reliability-policy.md gate matrix.

actor.description: What produced this reliability entry (e.g. script name, worker id, orchestrator).
